G-18 landed in commit fb15cb8: NonInteractiveFailure enum + ExitCodeFor mapping in cli/internal/moat/noninteractive.go, with 9 test functions covering stability, distinctness, reserved band, labels, and actionable messages. Four MOAT non-interactive failure classes map to exit codes 10-13. AD-9 updated to cite spec verbatim. Phase 2 merge (syllago-dsqjz) wires into registry-sync + install paths. Pre-approval deferred per MOAT ROADMAP Issue 11.

G-13 landed in commit 7d0f2a5: ContentEntry.TrustTier() now enforces the attestation_hash_mismatch downgrade defensively — when the flag is true, returns Signed (never Dual-Attested) regardless of signing_profile presence. 4-corner truth table covered in TestParseManifest_AttestationHashMismatchDowngrade plus direct unit test in TestContentEntry_TrustTierDowngradeDirect. Client MUST NOT hard-block satisfied by wiring only into the tier computation (which informs display, not install gating). Metadata panel badge wiring lands in Phase 2 merge (syllago-dsqjz) — it will read the same TrustTier() that now carries the downgrade authoritatively.

G-14 landed in cli/internal/moat/verify.go: CurrentPayloadVersion constant, SupportedPayloadVersions slice (currently [1]), IsSupportedPayloadVersion gate, CanonicalPayloadForVersion(v,h) builder that returns (nil,false) for unsupported versions. CanonicalPayloadFor now delegates to the versioned builder. item_verify.go documentation block makes the content-hash-first ordering (ADR 0007 G-14 / spec §Ordering) explicit. Syllago's architecture makes the TOCTOU attack structurally impossible — _version is never read from the wire. 6 new tests + all existing Rekor fixture anchors still pass. Grace-period dispatch lands with any actual schema bump in the future (no current bump pending).

G-15 landed in 2bd117c: added SyncRegistryRevocations(m) on Lockfile that merges registry-source revocations into revoked_hashes while preserving every existing entry. Publisher-source revocations are deliberately excluded (warn-once contract preserved, ADR 0007 G-8). The headline archival invariant is now locked by 7 new tests including a persistence-across-manifest-pruning scenario (syncing a manifest that no longer lists a previously-known revocation MUST NOT remove the lockfile entry — silent pruning would let a compromised registry un-revoke malicious content by dropping rows) and a Save/Load round-trip that proves the guarantee survives disk. Field comment on RevokedHashes and updated docstring on AddRevokedHash cite spec v0.6.0 §Revocation + ADR 0007 G-15 verbatim so future readers cannot misinterpret the append-only contract. Phase 2 big merge (syllago-dsqjz) wires SyncRegistryRevocations into the registry-sync flow; meta-registry-side tombstones enforcement is MR-11.

G-10 landed in 41243d9: added ContentEntry.IsPrivate(), Manifest.HasPrivateContent(), and Manifest.PrivateContent() — the explicit per-item visibility surface Phase 2 wiring reads when deciding whether a manifest requires bulk-install confirmation. ContentEntry.PrivateRepo bool already parsed per-item; the actual gap was the semantic contract plus test coverage proving per-item independence survives ParseManifest round-trip. Field doc on ContentEntry now spells out that the per-item publisher declaration supplants any registry-level probe fallback (ADR 0007 G-10). 4 new tests cover truth-table, nil receiver, mixed-visibility round-trip, HasPrivateContent across nil/empty/all-public/all-private/mixed, and PrivateContent order preservation. Phase 2 big merge (syllago-dsqjz) wires HasPrivateContent into the install/sync confirmation prompt.

Phase 2a CLI wrapper landed in commit 80303e6; primitive landed in 74e53b6. All G-18 exit codes (10/11/13) mapped, --yes flag wired, 11 tests covering every branch at 92.7% coverage on syncMOATRegistry. commands.json regenerated.

Gate primitives shipped in cli/internal/installer/moat_gate.go + moat_gate_test.go (94.4-100% coverage on new functions). Full <registry>/<content> end-to-end behavior deferred to follow-ups: syllago-kvf66 (catalog ContentItem MOAT fields) and syllago-svdwc (runInstall wiring + exit-code mapping). syllago-dsqjz big-merge now blocked on both new beads.

Shipped EnrichCatalog + FindContentEntry + moatTierToCatalogTier in cli/internal/moat/enrich.go with enrich_test.go at 100% coverage on all three functions (13 tests covering nil guards, tier mapping, G-13 downgrade, revocation population, registry-name filtering, duplicate revocations, mixed catalog). Re-scoped per design note — no ContentItem field additions needed.

Parse + sync + lookup slice of svdwc decomposition complete. install_moat.go (233 LOC) + install_moat_test.go (14 tests, parseRegistryItemSyntax 100%, runInstallFromRegistry 91.3%, printRegistryItemSummary 90%, shortHash 100%). All G-18 exit paths (10/11/13), VerifyError mapping, item-not-found, NotModified hint, and dry-run summary covered. Non-dry-run intentionally returns transitional MOAT_004 pointing to syllago-raivj. make test green.

Gate wiring complete. runInstallFromRegistry invokes installer.PreInstallCheck; resolveGateDecision switches across 5 MOATGateDecision branches with headless exit codes 10/12 and interactive Y/n prompts via moatInstallPromptFn seam. Telemetry moat_tier + moat_gated enriched. New error codes MOAT_008 and MOAT_009 with embedded docs. Proceed-path fetch deferred to sibling source-fetcher bead. Tests: 23+ cases, coverage runInstallFromRegistry 93 percent. Full make test green.

Source-fetcher landed. fetchAndRecord downloads HTTPS tarball from ContentEntry.SourceURI, verifies sha256 matches content_hash BEFORE any fs side-effect, extracts into ~/.cache/syllago/moat-sources/<registry>/<item>/<sha256-prefix>/ with path-traversal defense (rejects ..., absolute paths; skips symlinks), then calls installer.RecordInstall with nil bundle (UNSIGNED tier) plus lf.Save. SIGNED/DUAL-ATTESTED return MOAT_004 pending per-item Rekor bundle fetching in a follow-up. git+https returns MOAT_004 with clear follow-up guidance. Wired into resolveGateDecision Proceed branch in install_moat.go replacing the deferred-fetch error. Tests: 11 cases across download (ok, 404, oversize via fake RoundTripper), extract (ok, path-traversal x3 subtests, symlink skip), fetchAndRecord (happy unsigned with lockfile roundtrip, hash mismatch, signed-refusal, scheme-refusal, nil guards, corrupt gzip, unreachable host). Coverage: fetchAndRecord 88.9 percent, downloadTarball 77.8 percent, extractGzipTarball 64.9 percent. Full make test green, golangci-lint clean.

Integration tests cover 5 bk2vg scenarios end-to-end: clean UNSIGNED install + lockfile+cache persistence, registry-source revocation refusal (MOAT_008), publisher-warn headless exit 12, private-repo headless exit 10, tier-below-policy refusal (MOAT_009). All pass; committed b0b1e39.

All 4 sub-beads closed (elvv3/raivj/u128o/bk2vg). syllago install <registry>/<item> fully wired: parse, sync, gate (HardBlock/PublisherWarn/PrivatePrompt/TierBelowPolicy with G-18 exit codes 10/12), source-fetch (UNSIGNED tier only; HTTPS + sha256 + tar extract), RecordInstall + lockfile. Integration tests green. Unblocks syllago-2izhr + syllago-dsqjz.

Spec + findings docs written. Two rounds of sonnet-model review: first found 11 MUST-FIX symbol/signature errors, second found 3 MUST-FIX + 2 SHOULD-FIX issues. All resolved. Third review confirms CLEAN. Follow-up beads 2h86l/ym3pl/dwjcy/sn97y created for deferred SHOULD-FIX. Spec ready for lqas0 (plan bead).

Phase 2+3 complete. Plumbing + producer wiring shipped in d3c2b44. Producer live at internal/tui/app.go:228 (TUI rescan). Full suite green, moat package coverage 88.4%. Follow-up work (async rescan refactor, remaining cmd/ call-site migrations, app_update.go:408 discarded-cmd bug) tracked under syllago-nmjrm.

Shipped UX slice in fd260ce + adb6b69. Full gate-decision integration carved out as syllago-u0jna (now blocks syllago-dsqjz). Golden regeneration remains in syllago-0kbbx. hf5am no longer in-progress — what shipped is cohesive user-visible work; the deferred piece is a clean follow-up with its own acceptance criteria.
2c chain complete: plumbing (lqas0), presentation (hf5am), goldens+validation (0kbbx). Manual smoke handed to Holden as dsqjz gate.

Phase 2c complete via 4-bead chain: nyhgy (spec+expert review) → lqas0 (plumbing+producer wiring) → hf5am (TUI presentation + publisher-warn modal) → 0kbbx (goldens + CI gates). Unblocks syllago-dsqjz (Phase 2 big merge).

Added cli/internal/moat/item_verify_shard_index_test.go with 2 regression tests. TestBuildTransparencyLogEntry_PreservesShardLocalLogIndex pins the shard-local InclusionProof.LogIndex value (1214212107) directly; TestBuildTransparencyLogEntry_VerifyInclusionRejectsGlobalIndex constructs both a correct and a buggy TLE from the same fixture and verifies VerifyInclusion accepts the correct one while rejecting the buggy one (error surfaces as 'index is beyond size: 1336116369 >= 1215255133'). Verified regression behavior by temporarily replacing p.LogIndex with entry.LogIndex in buildTransparencyLogEntry — both tests failed with clear error messages, then both pass after reverting. Full moat suite green, make fmt + go vet + make build clean.

Added three partial-failure rollback tests to cli/internal/loadout/integration_test.go: TestApply_MultiItemRollback_HookFailureRevertsSymlinks (multi-type bundle, asserts no orphan symlinks + settings unchanged + snapshot gone), TestApply_PartialHookMergeRollback_RestoresSettingsJson (two hooks same-type deterministic order, first merges then second fails, asserts settings.json byte-exact restored), TestApply_MidBundleFailure_LaterItemsNeverApplied (three hooks with FIRST_MARKER / THIRD_MARKER sentinels proving items after failure never run). Verified regression behavior by temporarily stubbing the rollback block in apply.go — all three tests failed with clear messages (leftover symlinks, unrestored settings.json, FIRST_MARKER pollution). Reverted. Upgraded integration_test.go:398 hollow t.Logf finding covered.

Added four snapshot tests to cli/internal/snapshot/snapshot_test.go: TestRestore_MissingBackupFile_ReturnsError (deleted backup → Restore errors, target untouched); TestLoad_CorruptManifest_ReturnsParseError (invalid JSON manifest → parse error, not ErrNoSnapshot); TestRestore_TruncatedBackupFile_PinsCurrentCopyThroughBehavior (behavior pin: zero-byte backup silently zero-bytes target — data-loss gap documented); TestRestore_TargetReplacedWithSymlink_FollowsAndWritesThroughSymlink (behavior pin: TOCTOU symlink swap causes write-through to attacker file — documented, confined to test tempdir). Two tests are behavior pins documenting current gaps; both are deliberately written so a future hardening change (e.g. O_NOFOLLOW, hash verification) will flag the test for deliberate update. Full snapshot suite green. Gaps surfaced here should be filed as separate security/robustness beads if prioritized.

Added two end-to-end tests to cli/internal/updater/updater_test.go: TestUpdate_NoSigningKey_EmitsConservativeWarningEndToEnd (full Update flow with SigningPublicKey='' captures all progress messages and asserts the 'no signing key ... not verified' warning bubbles through to the caller AND no false 'Signature verified' message appears); TestUpdate_NoSigningKey_ChecksumStillEnforced (proves SHA-256 checksum mismatch still blocks update when signing key is absent — closes the 'silent skip all integrity' regression surface). Verified regression behavior by temporarily removing the progress warning call in verifyChecksumSignature — first test failed with clear message listing the emitted messages and flagging the missing warning. Reverted. The existing verifyChecksumSignature unit test only covers the warning inside that function; these tests prove it actually surfaces end-to-end.

Added E2E pipeline tests: TestEnrichCatalog_E2E_ParseManifestThroughDisplay covers the full display matrix (Dual-Attested/Signed/Unsigned/Recalled/missing-entry) driven by real ParseManifest; TestEnrichCatalog_E2E_HashMismatchDowngradesThroughJSON pins the G-13 downgrade through JSON. Regression verified by sabotaging EnrichCatalog and confirming clear, targeted failures.

Added 7 tests in rules_test.go exercising canonicalizeMarkdownRule via 'gemini-cli' and 'opencode' source providers. Covers: plain markdown happy path, YAML frontmatter preservation, empty-frontmatter defensive default, CRLF normalization, unicode body preservation, unclosed-frontmatter graceful degradation. Regression-verified with two separate sabotages (defensive-default skip + blanket alwaysApply=false) catching 1 and 4 test failures respectively.

Added 5 tests in cli/internal/installer/mcp_test.go covering Cursor + Windsurf MCP install paths via the existing mcpConfigPath test seam. Cursor: schema-merge happy path (asserts mcpServers.github.{command,args,env.GITHUB_TOKEN,type} with gjson paths), H2 conflict-refusal vs user-owned entry (user's command survives untouched), and allow-overwrite when installed.json marks entry as syllago-managed. Windsurf: schema-merge into mcpServers.local-py (command/args/env.PORT) and H2 conflict-refusal. Regression-verified with two sabotages: (1) disabled the !syllagoManaged guard — both conflict tests failed with targeted messages; (2) replaced SetRawBytes payload with {"command":"sabotaged"} — all 3 schema-merge/overwrite tests failed with per-field diagnostics. Also discovered and filed a follow-up: mcpConfigPathImpl has no case for 'cursor' or 'windsurf' slugs in production code, so real installs for these providers error with 'MCP config path not defined'.

Added TestFieldPreservation_Skills_FanOut in field_preservation_test.go — fan-out matrix across all 11 skill-capable providers (Claude Code, Gemini, OpenCode, Kiro, Cursor, Windsurf, Amp, Cline, RooCode, Copilot-CLI, Codex) with per-target assertContains/assertAbsent/minWarns/filename assertions. Pins two distinct field-handling strategies: (1) warnings-only providers (cursor/windsurf/gemini-cli/copilot-cli/kiro) route hooks through formatSkillHookWarnings → Result.Warnings, not prose; (2) prose-embed providers (amp/cline/roo-code/opencode) route hooks through buildSkillProseNotes as '**Hooks:**' text, no warnings. Also pins the tool-translation boundary: providers using TranslateTools emit native names (e.g. 'read_file' for Cursor), while buildSkillProseNotes emits canonical names (e.g. 'file_read') because it does not translate — this inconsistency is now frozen so future refactors surface it. Extended TestKitchenSinkSkillRoundTrip to include 5 new targets (amp, cline, roo-code, copilot-cli, codex); Zed excluded because it does not SupportsType(catalog.Skills). Regression-verified with two sabotages: (1) dropped buildSkillProseNotes for Amp — Amp subtest failed with 5 targeted contains-assertion failures (Tool restriction, file_read, search, Hooks:, syllago:converted); (2) dropped hook warnings for Copilot — Copilot subtest failed on minWarns: 1 assertion. Observed in code and now pinned by test: Codex has no case in SkillsConverter.Render switch and falls through to renderClaudeSkill (default), so Codex skill output is byte-identical to Claude Code's. If Codex gets its own renderer later, both the fan-out test and the round-trip test will surface the change.

Deleted four hollow tests in cmd/syllago/coverage_test.go: TestRunLoadoutList_NoLoadouts (t.Logf soft assertion), TestCheckAndWarnStaleSnapshot_NoSnapshot (no assertion), TestIsInteractiveImpl (discarded result), TestRunLoadoutApply_NoArgs_NoCatalog (t.Log soft assertion). Full cmd/syllago suite passes (4.371s).

Added semantic assertions to all 5 pure-golden TUI tests: TestGoldenGallery_80x30 (Python-Web + [a] Add loadout + absence of Add registry), TestGoldenGallery_120x40 (my-registry, alpha-skill, beta-rule, [a] Add registry, absence of Add loadout), TestGolden_RegistryAddModal_Buttons_80x30 (all four action buttons: [a] Add registry, [S] Sync, [d] Remove, [e] Edit), TestGolden_Triage_80x30 and _120x40 (shared assertTriageRender helper: step label, 5 item names, ✓ before pre-selected items, no ✓ before unchecked). Verified each catches regressions: sabotaged topbar.go to replace button labels — all 3 gallery tests flagged; sabotaged add_wizard_view.go to skip ✓ prefix — both triage tests flagged before golden mismatch fired. Full TUI suite passes (5.100s).

Replaced hollow len(result.Fields) > 0 check in TestTypeScriptExtractor_EnumWithNumbers with field-and-value map (Status.OK=200, Status.NotFound=404, Status.Error=500) plus landmark assertion. Extended TestFixtures_WindsurfLLMSTxt with 10 field-value assertions covering hooks.{PreToolUse,PostToolUse,Stop}.{description,blocking} and tools.{shell,file_read,file_write,file_edit}.native. Verified via sabotage: corrupted yaml extractor ScalarNode to always return 'corrupted' — all 10 Windsurf fields flagged with precise diagnostics; corrupted TS extractor to replace number values with 'BROKEN' — all 3 enum values flagged. Reverted both sabotages. Full capmon suite passes.

Added 8 adversarial tests to extract_markdown_test.go (11 total, up from 3): NoHeadings_NoPrimary, MismatchedHeadingLevel, MultiTable_TargetsLaterSection, UnicodeCells, InlineCodeInCell, MalformedTable_NoSeparator, UnevenColumns, NoHeadings_WithPrimary. Each case debug-inspected first to pin exact extractor behaviour (landmarks, field keys, sanitizer pass-through, goldmark table-parsing leniency). Verified via two sabotages: always-in-section → NoHeadings_WithPrimary flagged; dropped-level-check → MismatchedHeadingLevel flagged with diagnostic showing leaked row fields. Both sabotages reverted. Full extract_markdown suite passes.

TestUpdate_FullFlow rewritten to assert full success flow deterministically. Added osExecutable test seam to updater.go (var osExecutable = os.Executable) so tests can redirect the os.Rename target onto a disposable t.TempDir() file instead of silently clobbering the running test binary (Linux os.Rename succeeds against a running binary — the original test had been accidentally destructive). Test now asserts err==nil, all three progress messages ('Downloading…', 'Verifying checksum…', 'Updated to…'), and that the fake binary's content was actually replaced by the new binaryContent. Sabotage verified: inverted the checksum EqualFold guard — test flagged it with 'Update failed — checksum flow did not complete: checksum mismatch…'. Reverted cleanly, all 3 updater tests still pass, fmt+vet clean.

Removed cli/internal/signing/signing_test.go. Confirmed via grep -r: no package in the codebase imports internal/signing (its own header comment already states 'Nothing in the codebase imports this package currently'). All six tests were tautological: TestSignerInterfaceCompiles / TestVerifierInterfaceCompiles used 'var _ Signer = nil' which Go enforces at compile time; TestMethodConstants / TestTrustPolicyDefaults / TestSignatureBundleFields / TestRevocationListStructure just restate declared values and zero-values (Go language guarantees). These gave false confidence — when real implementations land they would report PASS without verifying behavior. Package still builds, go vet clean, make fmt clean. Option 2 (remove stubs) chosen over Option 1 (implement) because sigstore-go and go-crypto integration is gated on the larger hook provenance feature which has its own bead track. When implementations arrive, tests should be added with real behavioral assertions at that time.

Already resolved by prior refactoring. Verified via grep: (1) None of the three tests named in the bead (TestInstallFlagsRegistered, TestSyncAndExportFlagsDefined, TestSyncAndExportCommandRegisters) exist in the tree. (2) Zero *_test.go files under cli/ use cobra Flags().Lookup — the entire cobra wiring-check idiom has been removed. (3) Tests whose names brush against 'TestSync*' (moat/sync_test.go, moat/lockfile_test.go, registry/coverage_test.go, registry/registry_test.go) are behavioral — they exercise nil inputs, network paths, security hardening, and data sync logic, not flag registration. No action needed on this bead — the diagnostic was accurate at the time of audit (2026-04-20) but the pattern no longer appears in the codebase. Part of syllago-t5k5g audit epic.

Audited all 18 t.Log/t.Logf occurrences across cli/ test suite. Classified into actionable vs. informational:

ACTIONABLE SOFT-ASSERTIONS UPGRADED TO t.Errorf (4):
1. sandbox/coverage_test.go:349 (TestRunSession_PreflightCheckFails) — was logging when RunSession errored without 'pre-flight' or 'blocked' substrings. Sabotage verified: replacing projDir with a non-existent path produces 'directory safety check failed: cannot resolve path' which triggered the t.Errorf with clear diagnostic.
2. moat/sigstore_verify_test.go:84 (TestVerifyItemSigstore_RejectsWrongIdentity) — was logging when sigstore-go's rejection error lacked 'certificate'/'identity'/'SAN' keywords. Sabotage verified: replacing fmt.Errorf('sigstore-go verify: %w', err) with 'auth denied' in sigstore_spike_test.go caused t.Errorf to fire with the expected diagnostic.
3. moat/item_verify_shard_index_test.go:209 (TestBuildTransparencyLogEntry_VerifyInclusionRejectsGlobalIndex) — was logging when VerifyInclusion error lacked 'index'/'tree'/'inclusion'/'proof' keywords, which would mask shard-index bug regression. Sabotage verified with bogus substring placeholders ('xyzqq', 'bogusword') — t.Errorf fired with 'got: index is beyond size: 1336116369 >= 1215255133' diagnostic.
4. loadout/integration_test.go:712 (TestApplyConflict_ItemAlreadyInstalled) — was logging when snapshot.Load returned anything other than ErrNoSnapshot after a conflict abort, masking both 'conflict detection moved after snapshot creation' and 'rollback failed to clean up'. Sabotage verified: moved conflict check below snapshot.Create in apply.go — test fired at line 711 with 'snapshot should not exist after conflict abort … got: <nil>'.

INFORMATIONAL/DOCUMENTED LOGS LEFT ALONE (14):
- loadout/golden_test.go:26 and tui_v1/golden_test.go:44 — update-golden announcement (correct usage)
- converter/adapter_test.go:567 — logs returned warnings when present (informational)
- moat/hash_test.go:464 — documented 'keep green if only filename differs' per TV-15 spec intent
- catalog/scanner_index_integration_test.go:62 — informational warning dump
- capmon/extract_test.go:112 — placeholder for future live-network test
- capmon/seederspec_audit_test.go:61 — diagnostic after successful load
- sandbox/coverage_test.go:343 — legitimate skip when bwrap/socat/claude all available
- tui/wizard_shell_test.go:83 — skip when zone marks not active in env (could use t.Skip instead, but behavioral equivalent)
- telemetry/config_test.go:87 — skips when /etc/syllago/telemetry.json absent in test env (legitimate, though _=sc discard hides the nil-return contract; minor, not this bead)
- 4 matches in tui_v1/ — slated for deletion per syllago-fqi6l, not touched

Post-audit: make fmt + make vet clean; all 4 affected packages (sandbox, moat, loadout, and the 3 test files themselves) pass. Part of syllago-t5k5g.

Extracted clineMCPSettingsPathFor(goos, home, appdata) helper from ClineMCPSettingsPath so unit tests can exercise every runtime.GOOS branch in one test process — runtime.GOOS is a compile-time constant so without this refactor the alternate branches stay dark forever. Added TestClineMCPSettingsPathFor_AllPlatforms with 5 subtests: darwin/Library-Application-Support, windows with APPDATA set, windows with APPDATA unset (home/AppData/Roaming fallback), linux/.config, and a freebsd case to pin the default-branch behaviour (non-mac/win falls through to .config). Each subtest asserts the expected path segments AND guards against cross-branch leakage (e.g. darwin/windows paths must not contain '.config'). Coverage: clineMCPSettingsPathFor 100%, ClineMCPSettingsPath 75% (the remaining 25% is the os.UserHomeDir error guard, untestable without process-level env manipulation — acceptable for a fail-closed return ''). Sabotage verified: removed the 'if appdata == "" { appdata = home/AppData/Roaming }' fallback — the 'windows_with_APPDATA_unset' subtest fired with 'path "Code/User/globalStorage/..." missing expected segment "C:\Users\bob/AppData/Roaming"'. Reverted cleanly. Existing TestClineMCPSettingsPath behaviour preserved (platform-specific integration check kept for defence in depth). make fmt + vet clean, all provider tests pass. Part of syllago-t5k5g.

Added 3 payload-integration tests (TestEnrich_PropertiesReachPayload, TestEnrich_ScopeIsolation, TestResetEnrichment_ClearsPending) that stand up httptest.NewServer, fire Enrich()+TrackCommand() through the real PostHog code path, and assert the enriched properties reach the HTTP payload. Sabotage-verified all three: (1) skipped the enrichedProps merge in TrackCommand → payload-missing-property test fired naming all 4 missing keys; (2) skipped the post-merge clear → scope-leak test fired with 'SCOPE LEAK' diagnostic on both leaked keys; (3) made ResetEnrichment a no-op → clears-pending test fired naming unwrapped props. All sabotages reverted; make fmt vet and full telemetry suite pass clean.

Added cursor + windsurf cases to mcpConfigPathImpl (cli/internal/installer/mcp.go). Cursor → repoRoot/.cursor/mcp.json (project-local, matches other per-repo providers and cursor.go DiscoveryPaths); Windsurf → home/.codeium/windsurf/mcp_config.json (global, per docs.windsurf.com — no project-local alternative exists). Verified canonical paths via cursor.com/docs/context/mcp and docs.windsurf.com/windsurf/cascade/mcp.

Regression tests (mcp_test.go) that hit the real mcpConfigPathImpl without the test seam: (1) extended TestMCPConfigPath_ProjectScoped with cursor subtest; (2) added TestMCPConfigPath_HomeScoped for windsurf with HOME-derived path assertion; (3) TestInstallMCP_Cursor_ProductionPath runs full installMCP against a pre-created .cursor/mcp.json in a temp repoRoot; (4) TestInstallMCP_Windsurf_ProductionPath runs installMCP with t.Setenv HOME to a fake dir. Sabotage-verified by deleting both switch cases — all 4 tests fired with 'MCP config path not defined' and the diagnostic 'slug=cursor/windsurf no longer maps through mcpConfigPathImpl'. Reverted; make fmt vet + full installer suite pass.

Hardened cli/internal/snapshot/snapshot.go Restore against pre-Restore symlink swap. Added restoreToFile wrapper that os.Lstat's the destination first and returns ErrRestoreSymlinkTarget when the path is a symlink, short-circuiting before the OpenFile call that would otherwise follow the link. copyFile itself unchanged — still used for Create's writes into snapshotDir, where the threat shape doesn't apply.

Flipped the pin test: TestRestore_TargetReplacedWithSymlink_FollowsAndWritesThroughSymlink → TestRestore_TargetReplacedWithSymlink_Refuses. New assertions: (1) Restore returns an error that Is(ErrRestoreSymlinkTarget); (2) side file still holds sentinel content (no write followed the symlink); (3) target path is still the planted symlink (we did not clobber it).

Sabotage-verified by swapping restoreToFile call back to plain copyFile → test fired with 'Restore succeeded against a symlink target — TOCTOU hardening regressed'. Reverted. Loadout suite still green (downstream Restore caller). make fmt vet clean.

Known residual: the lstat→open window is still a narrow TOCTOU race — a portable close would need syscall.O_NOFOLLOW (Linux/macOS only) or openat2 with RESOLVE_NO_SYMLINKS. Current fix closes the pre-existing-symlink threat described in the bead; a deeper O_NOFOLLOW hardening can be a follow-up if a red team finds the window exploitable.

Hardened cli/internal/snapshot/snapshot.go Restore against truncated/corrupted backup files. Extended SnapshotManifest with BackedUpHashes map[string]string (omitempty, backwards-compatible with older manifests). Create now sha256's each backup file after writing it to snapshot/files and records the digest. Restore recomputes the hash per-entry and short-circuits with ErrRestoreCorruptBackup before restoreToFile ever touches the destination — so truncation or bit-rot leaves the post-apply content untouched.

Flipped the pin test: TestRestore_TruncatedBackupFile_PinsCurrentCopyThroughBehavior → TestRestore_TruncatedBackupFile_Refuses. New assertions: (1) Restore returns an error that Is(ErrRestoreCorruptBackup); (2) target file still holds post-apply content (verifies the refusal happens BEFORE the destination is opened).

Preserved the existing 'restoring X: ...' error prefix so TestRestore_MissingBackupFile_ReturnsError still passes its substring check. Sabotage-verified by replacing the hash check with a no-op → TestRestore_TruncatedBackupFile_Refuses fired with 'Restore succeeded with a truncated backup — integrity hardening regressed'. Reverted. Snapshot + loadout suites green; make fmt vet clean.

Added TestTrustedRootFixture_FreshnessWindow in cli/internal/moat/sigstore_verify_test.go — anchors the capture date of testdata/trusted-root-public-good.json in trustedRootCapturedAt (2026-04-17, sigstore-go v1.1.4 examples/trusted-root.json) and fails if the fixture has drifted more than trustedRootMaxAge (90 days). Error message names the capture date, age in days, max age in days, and the exact remediation (copy fresh fixture + bump the constant).

Sabotage-verified by setting trustedRootCapturedAt to 2025-01-01 → test fired with 'trusted-root-public-good.json is stale: captured ... (X days ago, max 90 days) — refresh from sigstore-go and bump trustedRootCapturedAt'. Reverted. make fmt vet clean; full moat suite passes.

Future behavior: whoever refreshes the Sigstore fixture bumps a one-line date constant. If they forget, this test fires 90 days later with a self-explanatory error — no separate CI job needed, no runbook dependency. Acceptance (2) from the bead.

Renamed six coverage_test.go files to names that describe their actual content, so readers don't dismiss them as coverage theater:

- cli/internal/provider/coverage_test.go → invariant_test.go (TestCoverageInternalGoConsistency is a true invariant checker: ConfigLocations[ct] and InstallDir(ct) must agree per provider).
- cli/internal/loadout/coverage_test.go → helpers_test.go (behavior tests for RefNames, NameRefsByType, and other helpers).
- cli/internal/sandbox/coverage_test.go → helpers_test.go (behavior tests including TestShouldSkipDiff).
- cli/internal/registry/coverage_test.go → probe_test.go (HTTP probe behavior tests via httpClient transport override).
- cli/internal/add/coverage_test.go → helpers_test.go (TestContentFilename and sibling helpers).
- cli/cmd/syllago/coverage_test.go → commands_test.go (71 tests covering command RunE functions; hollow stubs already removed in syllago-1547y, so rename is now accurate).

All via git mv to preserve history. Full test suite per affected package green; no remaining references to 'coverage_test' in the tree. Satisfies bead acceptance: 'Rename each such file to invariant_test.go (or similar descriptive name) where the content is actual invariant checking' — plus the 'after P2.1' condition for cmd/syllago that was already met.

Made the gap explicit via ErrUnimplementedDetectionMethod sentinel in CheckVersion. Content-hash and github-commits now return a wrapped sentinel instead of silently returning (nil, nil). Test rewritten as TestCheckVersion_UnimplementedMethods (table-driven, asserts sentinel via errors.Is with regression diagnostics naming the specific failure mode) plus TestCheckVersion_UnknownMethod (pins forward-compat for future method values). All three sabotages fired targeted errors. Full implementation deferred to syllago-5gthn.

Added four error-path tests: (1) TestNewLogger_ParentPathIsFile triggers MkdirAll failure via file-at-parent-path trick and asserts the 'creating audit log directory' wrap; (2) TestNewLogger_PathIsDirectory triggers OpenFile failure with a directory-as-file-path and asserts the 'opening audit log' wrap; (3) TestLogger_LogAfterClose_ReturnsErrorDoesNotPanic uses defer/recover to guard the no-panic contract and asserts a non-nil error is returned; (4) TestLogger_Log_PropagatesWriterError uses a deterministic failingWriter seam (stands in for disk-full) and asserts errors.Is(err, errFakeDiskFull). All three sabotages fired with targeted regression diagnostics. Remaining coverage gap: concurrent Close/Log race is not covered — outside this bead's scope.

Added four tests pinning the analyzer scoring constants and boundaries: (1) TestContentSignalDetector_ScoreConstants_Pinned locks contentSignalBase=0.40, contentSignalFloor=0.55, contentSignalCap=0.70 so any silent drift surfaces; (2) TestContentSignalDetector_ScoringInvariants asserts the two product guarantees — emit-floor >= skip-threshold and cap < auto-threshold — preventing partition regressions where items could silently drop or auto-install; (3) TestContentSignalDetector_ComputeScore_ExactValues pins exact numeric output for six input combinations covering base-only, each partition boundary, cap-exact, and overflow; (4) TestContentSignalDetector_ComputeScore_EmitFloorBoundary pins the just-below / AT / just-above emit-floor cases. Used epsilon comparison (1e-9) via floatsEqual helper since 0.40+0.20=0.6000000000000001 in IEEE 754 — matched production code which never compares summed floats directly. Three sabotages (shift base, raise cap past auto, drop floor below skip) all fired with targeted regression diagnostics.

Added cli/internal/tui/responsive_test.go with 5 tests complementing the size-variant goldens: (1) FramedLinesNeverExceedTerminal pins the MaxWidth clip via lipgloss.Width assertions on all lines inside the main frame; (2) LibraryColumnCount_ByWidth locks the w>=100 Description-column breakpoint by asserting 5 columns at 80x30 and 6 at 120x40; (3) MOATLibraryScope_TruncatesWithAsciiDots pins the ASCII '...' marker from truncate() (not U+2026) in the fixed-width 12-rune Scope column at both sizes; (4) LibraryNoTruncation_ShortScopes guards the opposite direction so <=12-char scopes never render '...'; (5) MOATLibraryScope_CountMatchesFixture asserts >=3 truncation occurrences matching the 3 long-registry MOAT fixture rows. Sabotage-verified: scope=20 fails #3+#5, scope=9 fails #4, w>=200 fails #2, MaxWidth removal from both wrapLine and renderHeader fails #1. Helpbar lines deliberately excluded — known overflow at narrow widths not in scope for this bead.

Added cross_model_transitions_test.go with 3 sabotage-verified tests covering all acceptance criteria. (1) InstallFlowPreservesLibraryTabAndSyncsItems: walks wizard-open → close → done → asserts Library tab stays active and library.table.items stays in sync with a.catalog.Items post-rescan. Sabotaged by removing library.SetItems in refreshContent → caught drift (1 vs 20). (2) AddWizardEscPreservesContentTab: opens add wizard on Content>Skills, presses Esc, drains addCloseMsg cmd, asserts topbar group/tab unchanged. Sabotaged by adding topBar.SetGroup(0) in addCloseMsg handler → caught the tab hijack (Content→Collections). (3) RefreshContentTargetsActiveSubModel: mutates catalog out-of-band on Content>Skills, calls (&app).refreshContent() directly (isolates dispatch from rescanCatalog's disk scan), asserts explorer received new item AND library baseline was NOT touched. Sabotaged two ways: leaking library.SetItems into the non-Library branch caught the leak (library went 7→8); removing explorer.SetItems caught missed dispatch at tab-switch precondition. Tests use package-private sub-model fields (library.table.items, explorer.items.items) which is fine in-package.

install_mouse_test.go (22 tests), add_wizard_mouse_test.go (13 tests), gallery_mouse_test.go (9 tests). All three acceptance criteria covered. Zones traced to source line numbers; sabotage-verified 6 critical paths across the three files (install button, provider row, add-src-0, add-exec-done, card click, meta-sync). scanZones() helper added to work around bubblezone v1.0.0 async goroutine race. 44 new mouse tests total, all passing at -race.
